To fully leverage temporal clues, we extend the TDMI to TDMI-ST where we replace the keyframe visual feature with the spatiotemporal feature of the input sequence to yield the final representation ˜Fi. The proposed TDMI-ST further pushes forward the performance boundary.
Our TDMI-ST improves mAP by 8.6 points over the adopted backbone network HRNet-W48.
Compared to the previous best-performed method FAMI-Pose, our TDMI-ST also delivers a 1.1 mAP gain.
The performance boost for challenging joints (i.e., wrist, ankle) is also encouraging: we obtain an mAP of 81.5 (↑ 1.5) for wrists and an mAP of 78.7 (↑ 1.7) for ankles.
